findings.description: Agenda content that is plain text in the PDF has been converted into a table in the HTML, changing the visual structure.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the schedule as aligned text lines with time, topic, and speaker separated by spacing and leader dots. The HTML renders the same content inside a bordered 3-column table with header row ('Time', 'Topic', 'Speaker').

findings.description: Title and heading styling differs noticeably from the PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title block is centered with smaller bold lines and compact spacing. In the HTML, the main title and subtitle are left-aligned, larger, and separated by a horizontal rule, creating a different visual emphasis.
